authors_text: Xiaoshi Wu,Keqiang Sun,Feng Zhu,Rui Zhao,Hongsheng Li
title: Human Preference Score: Better Aligning Text-to-Image Models with Human Preference
title_zh: 人类偏好评分：更好地将文本到图像模型与人类偏好保持一致
abstract: Recent years have witnessed a rapid growth of deep generative models, with text-to-image models gaining significant attention from the public. However, existing models often generate images that do not align well with human preferences, such as awkward combinations of limbs and facial expressions. To address this issue, we collect a dataset of human choices on generated images from the Stable Foundation Discord channel. Our experiments demonstrate that current evaluation metrics for generative models do not correlate well with human choices. Thus, we train a human preference classifier with the collected dataset and derive a Human Preference Score (HPS) based on the classifier. Using HPS, we propose a simple yet effective method to adapt Stable Diffusion to better align with human preferences. Our experiments show that HPS outperforms CLIP in predicting human choices and has good generalization capability toward images generated from other models. By tuning Stable Diffusion with the guidance of HPS, the adapted model is able to generate images that are more preferred by human users. The project page is available here: https://tgxs002.github.io/align_sd_web/ .
summary: Recent years have witnessed a rapid growth of deep generative models, with text-to-image models gaining significant attention from the public. However, existing models often generate images that do not align well with human preferences, such as awkward combinations of limbs and facial expressions. To address this issue, we collect a dataset of human choices on generated images from the Stable Foundation Discord channel. Our experiments demonstrate that current evaluation metrics for generative models do not correlate well with human choices. Thus, we train a human preference classifier with the collected dataset and derive a Human Preference Score (HPS) based on the classifier. Using HPS, we propose a simple yet effective method to adapt Stable Diffusion to better align with human preferences. Our experiments show that HPS outperforms CLIP in predicting human choices and has good generalization capability toward images generated from other models. By tuning Stable Diffusion with the guidance of HPS, the adapted model is able to generate images that are more preferred by human users. The project page is available here: https://tgxs002.github.io/align_sd_web/ .
abstract_zh: 近年来，深度生成模型迅速发展，文本到图像模型受到了公众的广泛关注。然而，现有模型经常生成与人类偏好不太相符的图像，例如四肢和面部表情的尴尬组合。为了解决这个问题，我们从 Stable Foundation Discord 频道收集了人类对生成图像的选择数据集。我们的实验表明，当前生成模型的评估指标与人类的选择并没有很好的相关性。因此，我们使用收集的数据集训练人类偏好分类器，并根据分类器得出人类偏好分数（HPS）。使用 HPS，我们提出了一种简单而有效的方法来调整稳定扩散，以更好地符合人类偏好。我们的实验表明，HPS 在预测人类选择方面优于 CLIP，并且对其他模型生成的图像具有良好的泛化能力。通过在 HPS 的指导下调整稳定扩散，适应的模型能够生成人类用户更喜欢的图像。项目页面位于：https://tgxs002.github.io/align_sd_web/。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于人类偏好的评分方法（HPS），用于改进文本到图像模型与人类偏好的对齐效果。
AI.motivation: 现有文本到图像模型生成的图像常与人类偏好不一致（如肢体和面部表情不协调），且现有评估指标无法有效反映人类选择。
AI.method: 通过收集Stable Foundation Discord频道中的人类选择数据，训练人类偏好分类器并构建HPS指标，利用HPS指导Stable Diffusion模型调优。
AI.result: HPS在预测人类选择上优于CLIP，且对其他模型生成的图像具有良好的泛化能力；基于HPS调优后的模型生成的图像更符合人类偏好。
AI.conclusion: HPS能有效提升文本到图像模型与人类偏好的对齐效果，具有跨模型的泛化能力，项目页面提供进一步验证。

authors_text: Han Xue,Zhiwu Huang,Qianru Sun,Li Song,Wenjun Zhang
title: Freestyle Layout-to-Image Synthesis
title_zh: 自由式布局到图像合成
abstract: Typical layout-to-image synthesis (LIS) models generate images for a closed set of semantic classes, e.g., 182 common objects in COCO-Stuff. In this work, we explore the freestyle capability of the model, i.e., how far can it generate unseen semantics (e.g., classes, attributes, and styles) onto a given layout, and call the task Freestyle LIS (FLIS). Thanks to the development of large-scale pre-trained language-image models, a number of discriminative models (e.g., image classification and object detection) trained on limited base classes are empowered with the ability of unseen class prediction. Inspired by this, we opt to leverage large-scale pre-trained text-to-image diffusion models to achieve the generation of unseen semantics. The key challenge of FLIS is how to enable the diffusion model to synthesize images from a specific layout which very likely violates its pre-learned knowledge, e.g., the model never sees "a unicorn sitting on a bench" during its pre-training. To this end, we introduce a new module called Rectified Cross-Attention (RCA) that can be conveniently plugged in the diffusion model to integrate semantic masks. This "plug-in" is applied in each cross-attention layer of the model to rectify the attention maps between image and text tokens. The key idea of RCA is to enforce each text token to act on the pixels in a specified region, allowing us to freely put a wide variety of semantics from pre-trained knowledge (which is general) onto the given layout (which is specific). Extensive experiments show that the proposed diffusion network produces realistic and freestyle layout-to-image generation results with diverse text inputs, which has a high potential to spawn a bunch of interesting applications. Code is available at https://github.com/essunny310/FreestyleNet.
summary: Typical layout-to-image synthesis (LIS) models generate images for a closed set of semantic classes, e.g., 182 common objects in COCO-Stuff. In this work, we explore the freestyle capability of the model, i.e., how far can it generate unseen semantics (e.g., classes, attributes, and styles) onto a given layout, and call the task Freestyle LIS (FLIS). Thanks to the development of large-scale pre-trained language-image models, a number of discriminative models (e.g., image classification and object detection) trained on limited base classes are empowered with the ability of unseen class prediction. Inspired by this, we opt to leverage large-scale pre-trained text-to-image diffusion models to achieve the generation of unseen semantics. The key challenge of FLIS is how to enable the diffusion model to synthesize images from a specific layout which very likely violates its pre-learned knowledge, e.g., the model never sees "a unicorn sitting on a bench" during its pre-training. To this end, we introduce a new module called Rectified Cross-Attention (RCA) that can be conveniently plugged in the diffusion model to integrate semantic masks. This "plug-in" is applied in each cross-attention layer of the model to rectify the attention maps between image and text tokens. The key idea of RCA is to enforce each text token to act on the pixels in a specified region, allowing us to freely put a wide variety of semantics from pre-trained knowledge (which is general) onto the given layout (which is specific). Extensive experiments show that the proposed diffusion network produces realistic and freestyle layout-to-image generation results with diverse text inputs, which has a high potential to spawn a bunch of interesting applications. Code is available at https://github.com/essunny310/FreestyleNet.
abstract_zh: 典型的布局到图像合成 (LIS) 模型为一组封闭的语义类生成图像，例如 COCO-Stuff 中的 182 个常见对象。在这项工作中，我们探索模型的自由式功能，即它能在给定布局上生成看不见的语义（例如类、属性和样式）到什么程度，并将任务称为自由式 LIS (FLIS)。由于大规模预训练语言图像模型的发展，许多在有限基类上训练的判别模型（例如图像分类和目标检测）被赋予了看不见的类别预测的能力。受此启发，我们选择利用大规模预训练的文本到图像扩散模型来实现看不见的语义的生成。 FLIS 的关键挑战是如何使扩散模型能够从特定布局合成图像，这很可能违反其预先学习的知识，例如，该模型在预训练期间从未看到“坐在长凳上的独角兽”。为此，我们引入了一个名为整流交叉注意（RCA）的新模块，可以方便地插入扩散模型中以集成语义掩码。该“插件”应用于模型的每个交叉注意力层，以纠正图像和文本标记之间的注意力图。 RCA 的关键思想是强制每个文本标记作用于指定区域中的像素，允许我们自由地将预训练知识（通用）中的各种语义放入给定布局（特定）上。大量的实验表明，所提出的扩散网络可以通过不同的文本输入产生逼真且自由式的布局到图像生成结果，这很有可能催生一系列有趣的应用程序。代码可在 https://github.com/essunny310/FreestyleNet 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Freestyle LIS（FLIS）的新任务，旨在通过给定布局生成包含未见过语义（如新类别、属性和风格）的图像。
AI.motivation: 传统布局到图像合成模型仅能处理预定义的语义类别，无法生成未见过的语义。受大规模预训练语言-图像模型在未知类别预测中的启发，本文希望利用扩散模型实现更自由的语义生成。
AI.method: 提出一种可插拔模块Rectified Cross-Attention (RCA)，通过修正扩散模型中图像与文本token的注意力图，使文本token作用于布局指定区域，从而将预训练知识中的通用语义映射到特定布局。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法能生成具有多样文本输入的逼真图像，展示了在未见过语义生成上的潜力，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: FLIS通过RCA模块有效解决了布局与预训练知识的对齐问题，为生成包含新颖语义的图像提供了新思路，具有广泛的应用前景。

ai_content.motivation: 传统布局到图像合成模型仅能处理预定义的语义类别，无法生成未见过的语义。受大规模预训练语言-图像模型在未知类别预测中的启发，本文希望利用扩散模型实现更自由的语义生成。
ai_content.method: 提出一种可插拔模块Rectified Cross-Attention (RCA)，通过修正扩散模型中图像与文本token的注意力图，使文本token作用于布局指定区域，从而将预训练知识中的通用语义映射到特定布局。
ai_content.result: 实验表明，该方法能生成具有多样文本输入的逼真图像，展示了在未见过语义生成上的潜力，代码已开源。
ai_content.conclusion: FLIS通过RCA模块有效解决了布局与预训练知识的对齐问题，为生成包含新颖语义的图像提供了新思路，具有广泛的应用前景。

search_text: Freestyle Layout-to-Image Synthesis 自由式布局到图像合成 Typical layout-to-image synthesis (LIS) models generate images for a closed set of semantic classes, e.g., 182 common objects in COCO-Stuff. In this work, we explore the freestyle capability of the model, i.e., how far can it generate unseen semantics (e.g., classes, attributes, and styles) onto a given layout, and call the task Freestyle LIS (FLIS). Thanks to the development of large-scale pre-trained language-image models, a number of discriminative models (e.g., image classification and object detection) trained on limited base classes are empowered with the ability of unseen class prediction. Inspired by this, we opt to leverage large-scale pre-trained text-to-image diffusion models to achieve the generation of unseen semantics. The key challenge of FLIS is how to enable the diffusion model to synthesize images from a specific layout which very likely violates its pre-learned knowledge, e.g., the model never sees "a unicorn sitting on a bench" during its pre-training. To this end, we introduce a new module called Rectified Cross-Attention (RCA) that can be conveniently plugged in the diffusion model to integrate semantic masks. This "plug-in" is applied in each cross-attention layer of the model to rectify the attention maps between image and text tokens. The key idea of RCA is to enforce each text token to act on the pixels in a specified region, allowing us to freely put a wide variety of semantics from pre-trained knowledge (which is general) onto the given layout (which is specific). Extensive experiments show that the proposed diffusion network produces realistic and freestyle layout-to-image generation results with diverse text inputs, which has a high potential to spawn a bunch of interesting applications. Code is available at https://github.com/essunny310/FreestyleNet. 典型的布局到图像合成 (LIS) 模型为一组封闭的语义类生成图像，例如 COCO-Stuff 中的 182 个常见对象。在这项工作中，我们探索模型的自由式功能，即它能在给定布局上生成看不见的语义（例如类、属性和样式）到什么程度，并将任务称为自由式 LIS (FLIS)。由于大规模预训练语言图像模型的发展，许多在有限基类上训练的判别模型（例如图像分类和目标检测）被赋予了看不见的类别预测的能力。受此启发，我们选择利用大规模预训练的文本到图像扩散模型来实现看不见的语义的生成。 FLIS 的关键挑战是如何使扩散模型能够从特定布局合成图像，这很可能违反其预先学习的知识，例如，该模型在预训练期间从未看到“坐在长凳上的独角兽”。为此，我们引入了一个名为整流交叉注意（RCA）的新模块，可以方便地插入扩散模型中以集成语义掩码。该“插件”应用于模型的每个交叉注意力层，以纠正图像和文本标记之间的注意力图。 RCA 的关键思想是强制每个文本标记作用于指定区域中的像素，允许我们自由地将预训练知识（通用）中的各种语义放入给定布局（特定）上。大量的实验表明，所提出的扩散网络可以通过不同的文本输入产生逼真且自由式的布局到图像生成结果，这很有可能催生一系列有趣的应用程序。代码可在 https://github.com/essunny310/FreestyleNet 获取。 本文提出一种名为Freestyle LIS（FLIS）的新任务，旨在通过给定布局生成包含未见过语义（如新类别、属性和风格）的图像。 Han Xue,Zhiwu Huang,Qianru Sun,Li Song,Wenjun Zhang cs.CV

authors_text: Leonardo Iurada,Silvia Bucci,Timothy M. Hospedales,Tatiana Tommasi
title: Fairness meets Cross-Domain Learning: a new perspective on Models and Metrics
title_zh: 公平遇见跨领域学习：模型和指标的新视角
abstract: Deep learning-based recognition systems are deployed at scale for several real-world applications that inevitably involve our social life. Although being of great support when making complex decisions, they might capture spurious data correlations and leverage sensitive attributes (e.g. age, gender, ethnicity). How to factor out this information while keeping a high prediction performance is a task with still several open questions, many of which are shared with those of the domain adaptation and generalization literature which focuses on avoiding visual domain biases. In this work, we propose an in-depth study of the relationship between cross-domain learning (CD) and model fairness by introducing a benchmark on face and medical images spanning several demographic groups as well as classification and localization tasks. After having highlighted the limits of the current evaluation metrics, we introduce a new Harmonic Fairness (HF) score to assess jointly how fair and accurate every model is with respect to a reference baseline. Our study covers 14 CD approaches alongside three state-of-the-art fairness algorithms and shows how the former can outperform the latter. Overall, our work paves the way for a more systematic analysis of fairness problems in computer vision. Code available at: https://github.com/iurada/fairness_crossdomain
summary: Deep learning-based recognition systems are deployed at scale for several real-world applications that inevitably involve our social life. Although being of great support when making complex decisions, they might capture spurious data correlations and leverage sensitive attributes (e.g. age, gender, ethnicity). How to factor out this information while keeping a high prediction performance is a task with still several open questions, many of which are shared with those of the domain adaptation and generalization literature which focuses on avoiding visual domain biases. In this work, we propose an in-depth study of the relationship between cross-domain learning (CD) and model fairness by introducing a benchmark on face and medical images spanning several demographic groups as well as classification and localization tasks. After having highlighted the limits of the current evaluation metrics, we introduce a new Harmonic Fairness (HF) score to assess jointly how fair and accurate every model is with respect to a reference baseline. Our study covers 14 CD approaches alongside three state-of-the-art fairness algorithms and shows how the former can outperform the latter. Overall, our work paves the way for a more systematic analysis of fairness problems in computer vision. Code available at: https://github.com/iurada/fairness_crossdomain
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的识别系统被大规模部署用于不可避免地涉及我们的社交生活的几个现实世界应用程序。尽管在做出复杂决策时提供了很大的支持，但它们可能会捕获虚假的数据相关性并利用敏感属性（例如年龄、性别、种族）。如何在保持高预测性能的同时提取这些信息是一项仍有几个悬而未决的问题的任务，其中许多问题与专注于避免视觉领域偏差的领域适应和泛化文献相同。在这项工作中，我们提出通过引入跨越多个人口群体的人脸和医学图像基准以及分类和本地化任务，深入研究跨领域学习（CD）和模型公平性之间的关系。在强调了当前评估指标的局限性之后，我们引入了新的谐波公平性（HF）分数来共同评估每个模型相对于参考基线的公平性和准确性。我们的研究涵盖了 14 种 CD 方法以及三种最先进的公平算法，并展示了前者如何优于后者。总的来说，我们的工作为更系统地分析计算机视觉中的公平问题铺平了道路。代码位于：https://github.com/iurada/fairness_crossdomain

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了跨领域学习与模型公平性的关系，提出新的评估指标和谐公平性（HF）并构建基准测试。
AI.motivation: 深度学习系统在现实应用中可能捕捉到敏感属性的虚假关联，需在保持性能的同时消除偏见，而跨领域学习与公平性问题存在共性。
AI.method: 构建包含人脸和医疗图像的基准数据集，涵盖多个群体及任务；提出结合公平性与准确性的HF指标，并对比14种跨领域方法与3种公平性算法。
AI.result: 跨领域方法在公平性与性能上优于现有公平性算法，HF指标能有效评估模型表现。
AI.conclusion: 跨领域学习为计算机视觉中的公平性分析提供了系统性视角，HF指标可促进该领域研究。

authors_text: Denis Kuznedelev,Soroush Tabesh,Kimia Noorbakhsh,Elias Frantar,Sara Beery,Eldar Kurtic,Dan Alistarh
title: Vision Models Can Be Efficiently Specialized via Few-Shot Task-Aware Compression
title_zh: 通过少样本任务感知压缩可以有效地专业化视觉模型
abstract: Recent vision architectures and self-supervised training methods enable vision models that are extremely accurate and general, but come with massive parameter and computational costs. In practical settings, such as camera traps, users have limited resources, and may fine-tune a pretrained model on (often limited) data from a small set of specific categories of interest. These users may wish to make use of modern, highly-accurate models, but are often computationally constrained. To address this, we ask: can we quickly compress large generalist models into accurate and efficient specialists? For this, we propose a simple and versatile technique called Few-Shot Task-Aware Compression (TACO). Given a large vision model that is pretrained to be accurate on a broad task, such as classification over ImageNet-22K, TACO produces a smaller model that is accurate on specialized tasks, such as classification across vehicle types or animal species. Crucially, TACO works in few-shot fashion, i.e. only a few task-specific samples are used, and the procedure has low computational overheads. We validate TACO on highly-accurate ResNet, ViT/DeiT, and ConvNeXt models, originally trained on ImageNet, LAION, or iNaturalist, which we specialize and compress to a diverse set of "downstream" subtasks. TACO can reduce the number of non-zero parameters in existing models by up to 20x relative to the original models, leading to inference speedups of up to 3$\times$, while remaining accuracy-competitive with the uncompressed models on the specialized tasks.
summary: Recent vision architectures and self-supervised training methods enable vision models that are extremely accurate and general, but come with massive parameter and computational costs. In practical settings, such as camera traps, users have limited resources, and may fine-tune a pretrained model on (often limited) data from a small set of specific categories of interest. These users may wish to make use of modern, highly-accurate models, but are often computationally constrained. To address this, we ask: can we quickly compress large generalist models into accurate and efficient specialists? For this, we propose a simple and versatile technique called Few-Shot Task-Aware Compression (TACO). Given a large vision model that is pretrained to be accurate on a broad task, such as classification over ImageNet-22K, TACO produces a smaller model that is accurate on specialized tasks, such as classification across vehicle types or animal species. Crucially, TACO works in few-shot fashion, i.e. only a few task-specific samples are used, and the procedure has low computational overheads. We validate TACO on highly-accurate ResNet, ViT/DeiT, and ConvNeXt models, originally trained on ImageNet, LAION, or iNaturalist, which we specialize and compress to a diverse set of "downstream" subtasks. TACO can reduce the number of non-zero parameters in existing models by up to 20x relative to the original models, leading to inference speedups of up to 3$\times$, while remaining accuracy-competitive with the uncompressed models on the specialized tasks.
abstract_zh: 最近的视觉架构和自监督训练方法使得视觉模型极其准确和通用，但伴随着大量的参数和计算成本。在实际设置中，例如相机陷阱，用户的资源有限，并且可以根据一小部分感兴趣的特定类别的（通常有限的）数据对预训练模型进行微调。这些用户可能希望利用现代的、高精度的模型，但通常受到计算的限制。为了解决这个问题，我们要问：我们能否快速将大型通才模型压缩为准确高效的专家模型？为此，我们提出了一种简单且通用的技术，称为少样本任务感知压缩（TACO）。给定一个经过预训练的大型视觉模型，可以在广泛的任务（例如 ImageNet-22K 上的分类）上保持准确，TACO 会生成一个较小的模型，该模型可以在特定的任务（例如跨车辆类型或动物物种的分类）上保持准确。至关重要的是，TACO 以少样本方式工作，即仅使用少数特定于任务的样本，并且该过程的计算开销较低。我们在高精度 ResNet、ViT/DeiT 和 ConvNeXt 模型上验证 TACO，这些模型最初是在 ImageNet、LAION 或 iNaturalist 上训练的，我们将其专门化并压缩为一组不同的“下游”子任务。相对于原始模型，TACO 可以将现有模型中的非零参数数量减少多达 20 倍，从而使推理速度提高高达 3$\times$，同时在专门任务上与未压缩模型保持准确性竞争。

AI.tldr: 提出一种名为TACO的方法，通过少量样本将大型通用视觉模型高效压缩为特定任务的专用模型。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉模型虽准确但计算成本高，实际场景中用户受限于资源需将通用模型快速适配为特定任务模型，需解决计算效率与精度平衡问题。
AI.method: 设计Few-Shot Task-Aware Compression（TACO）技术，在少量任务样本下通过参数压缩和任务感知优化，将预训练大模型转化为轻量专用模型。
AI.result: 在ResNet、ViT/DeiT等模型上验证，参数量减少20倍，推理速度提升3倍，且在特定任务上保持与原模型相当的精度。
AI.conclusion: TACO实现了低样本、低成本将通用视觉模型压缩为高效专用模型，为资源受限场景提供可行解决方案。

ai_content.tldr: 提出一种名为TACO的方法，通过少量样本将大型通用视觉模型高效压缩为特定任务的专用模型。
ai_content.motivation: 现有视觉模型虽准确但计算成本高，实际场景中用户受限于资源需将通用模型快速适配为特定任务模型，需解决计算效率与精度平衡问题。
ai_content.method: 设计Few-Shot Task-Aware Compression（TACO）技术，在少量任务样本下通过参数压缩和任务感知优化，将预训练大模型转化为轻量专用模型。
ai_content.result: 在ResNet、ViT/DeiT等模型上验证，参数量减少20倍，推理速度提升3倍，且在特定任务上保持与原模型相当的精度。

search_text: Vision Models Can Be Efficiently Specialized via Few-Shot Task-Aware Compression 通过少样本任务感知压缩可以有效地专业化视觉模型 Recent vision architectures and self-supervised training methods enable vision models that are extremely accurate and general, but come with massive parameter and computational costs. In practical settings, such as camera traps, users have limited resources, and may fine-tune a pretrained model on (often limited) data from a small set of specific categories of interest. These users may wish to make use of modern, highly-accurate models, but are often computationally constrained. To address this, we ask: can we quickly compress large generalist models into accurate and efficient specialists? For this, we propose a simple and versatile technique called Few-Shot Task-Aware Compression (TACO). Given a large vision model that is pretrained to be accurate on a broad task, such as classification over ImageNet-22K, TACO produces a smaller model that is accurate on specialized tasks, such as classification across vehicle types or animal species. Crucially, TACO works in few-shot fashion, i.e. only a few task-specific samples are used, and the procedure has low computational overheads. We validate TACO on highly-accurate ResNet, ViT/DeiT, and ConvNeXt models, originally trained on ImageNet, LAION, or iNaturalist, which we specialize and compress to a diverse set of "downstream" subtasks. TACO can reduce the number of non-zero parameters in existing models by up to 20x relative to the original models, leading to inference speedups of up to 3$\times$, while remaining accuracy-competitive with the uncompressed models on the specialized tasks. 最近的视觉架构和自监督训练方法使得视觉模型极其准确和通用，但伴随着大量的参数和计算成本。在实际设置中，例如相机陷阱，用户的资源有限，并且可以根据一小部分感兴趣的特定类别的（通常有限的）数据对预训练模型进行微调。这些用户可能希望利用现代的、高精度的模型，但通常受到计算的限制。为了解决这个问题，我们要问：我们能否快速将大型通才模型压缩为准确高效的专家模型？为此，我们提出了一种简单且通用的技术，称为少样本任务感知压缩（TACO）。给定一个经过预训练的大型视觉模型，可以在广泛的任务（例如 ImageNet-22K 上的分类）上保持准确，TACO 会生成一个较小的模型，该模型可以在特定的任务（例如跨车辆类型或动物物种的分类）上保持准确。至关重要的是，TACO 以少样本方式工作，即仅使用少数特定于任务的样本，并且该过程的计算开销较低。我们在高精度 ResNet、ViT/DeiT 和 ConvNeXt 模型上验证 TACO，这些模型最初是在 ImageNet、LAION 或 iNaturalist 上训练的，我们将其专门化并压缩为一组不同的“下游”子任务。相对于原始模型，TACO 可以将现有模型中的非零参数数量减少多达 20 倍，从而使推理速度提高高达 3$\times$，同时在专门任务上与未压缩模型保持准确性竞争。 提出一种名为TACO的方法，通过少量样本将大型通用视觉模型高效压缩为特定任务的专用模型。 Denis Kuznedelev,Soroush Tabesh,Kimia Noorbakhsh,Elias Frantar,Sara Beery,Eldar Kurtic,Dan Alistarh cs.CV

authors_text: Ziqin Wang,Bowen Cheng,Lichen Zhao,Dong Xu,Yang Tang,Lu Sheng
title: VL-SAT: Visual-Linguistic Semantics Assisted Training for 3D Semantic Scene Graph Prediction in Point Cloud
title_zh: VL-SAT：点云中 3D 语义场景图预测的视觉语言语义辅助训练
abstract: The task of 3D semantic scene graph (3DSSG) prediction in the point cloud is challenging since (1) the 3D point cloud only captures geometric structures with limited semantics compared to 2D images, and (2) long-tailed relation distribution inherently hinders the learning of unbiased prediction. Since 2D images provide rich semantics and scene graphs are in nature coped with languages, in this study, we propose Visual-Linguistic Semantics Assisted Training (VL-SAT) scheme that can significantly empower 3DSSG prediction models with discrimination about long-tailed and ambiguous semantic relations. The key idea is to train a powerful multi-modal oracle model to assist the 3D model. This oracle learns reliable structural representations based on semantics from vision, language, and 3D geometry, and its benefits can be heterogeneously passed to the 3D model during the training stage. By effectively utilizing visual-linguistic semantics in training, our VL-SAT can significantly boost common 3DSSG prediction models, such as SGFN and SGGpoint, only with 3D inputs in the inference stage, especially when dealing with tail relation triplets. Comprehensive evaluations and ablation studies on the 3DSSG dataset have validated the effectiveness of the proposed scheme. Code is available at https://github.com/wz7in/CVPR2023-VLSAT.
summary: The task of 3D semantic scene graph (3DSSG) prediction in the point cloud is challenging since (1) the 3D point cloud only captures geometric structures with limited semantics compared to 2D images, and (2) long-tailed relation distribution inherently hinders the learning of unbiased prediction. Since 2D images provide rich semantics and scene graphs are in nature coped with languages, in this study, we propose Visual-Linguistic Semantics Assisted Training (VL-SAT) scheme that can significantly empower 3DSSG prediction models with discrimination about long-tailed and ambiguous semantic relations. The key idea is to train a powerful multi-modal oracle model to assist the 3D model. This oracle learns reliable structural representations based on semantics from vision, language, and 3D geometry, and its benefits can be heterogeneously passed to the 3D model during the training stage. By effectively utilizing visual-linguistic semantics in training, our VL-SAT can significantly boost common 3DSSG prediction models, such as SGFN and SGGpoint, only with 3D inputs in the inference stage, especially when dealing with tail relation triplets. Comprehensive evaluations and ablation studies on the 3DSSG dataset have validated the effectiveness of the proposed scheme. Code is available at https://github.com/wz7in/CVPR2023-VLSAT.
abstract_zh: 点云中的 3D 语义场景图（3DSSG）预测任务具有挑战性，因为（1）与 2D 图像相比，3D 点云仅捕获语义有限的几何结构，（2）长尾关系分布本质上阻碍了无偏预测的学习。由于 2D 图像提供了丰富的语义，而场景图本质上是与语言打交道的，因此在本研究中，我们提出了视觉语言语义辅助训练（VL-SAT）方案，该方案可以显着增强 3DSSG 预测模型对长尾和模糊语义关系的区分能力。关键思想是训练一个强大的多模态预言机模型来辅助 3D 模型。该预言机基于视觉、语言和 3D 几何的语义学习可靠的结构表示，其好处可以在训练阶段异构地传递给 3D 模型。通过在训练中有效利用视觉语言语义，我们的 VL-SAT 可以显着增强常见的 3DSSG 预测模型，例如 SGFN 和 SGGpoint，仅在推理阶段使用 3D 输入，特别是在处理尾部关系三元组时。对3DSSG数据集的综合评估和消融研究验证了所提出方案的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/wz7in/CVPR2023-VLSAT 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出VL-SAT方法，通过视觉-语言语义辅助训练，解决点云中3D语义场景图预测的挑战。
AI.motivation: 点云数据语义信息有限且关系分布不均衡，导致3D语义场景图预测模型难以学习长尾和模糊语义关系。
AI.method: 构建多模态oracle模型，融合视觉、语言和3D几何语义，在训练阶段异构传递结构表示以增强3D模型。
AI.result: 在3DSSG数据集上验证了VL-SAT有效性，显著提升SGFN和SGGpoint等模型对尾部关系三元组的预测性能。
AI.conclusion: VL-SAT通过跨模态语义辅助训练，有效解决3D语义场景图预测中的长尾关系问题，代码已开源。

search_text: VL-SAT: Visual-Linguistic Semantics Assisted Training for 3D Semantic Scene Graph Prediction in Point Cloud VL-SAT：点云中 3D 语义场景图预测的视觉语言语义辅助训练 The task of 3D semantic scene graph (3DSSG) prediction in the point cloud is challenging since (1) the 3D point cloud only captures geometric structures with limited semantics compared to 2D images, and (2) long-tailed relation distribution inherently hinders the learning of unbiased prediction. Since 2D images provide rich semantics and scene graphs are in nature coped with languages, in this study, we propose Visual-Linguistic Semantics Assisted Training (VL-SAT) scheme that can significantly empower 3DSSG prediction models with discrimination about long-tailed and ambiguous semantic relations. The key idea is to train a powerful multi-modal oracle model to assist the 3D model. This oracle learns reliable structural representations based on semantics from vision, language, and 3D geometry, and its benefits can be heterogeneously passed to the 3D model during the training stage. By effectively utilizing visual-linguistic semantics in training, our VL-SAT can significantly boost common 3DSSG prediction models, such as SGFN and SGGpoint, only with 3D inputs in the inference stage, especially when dealing with tail relation triplets. Comprehensive evaluations and ablation studies on the 3DSSG dataset have validated the effectiveness of the proposed scheme. authors_text: Yiqian Wu,Jing Zhang,Hongbo Fu,Xiaogang Jin
title: LPFF: A Portrait Dataset for Face Generators Across Large Poses
title_zh: LPFF：用于大姿势的面部生成器的肖像数据集
abstract: The creation of 2D realistic facial images and 3D face shapes using generative networks has been a hot topic in recent years. Existing face generators exhibit exceptional performance on faces in small to medium poses (with respect to frontal faces) but struggle to produce realistic results for large poses. The distorted rendering results on large poses in 3D-aware generators further show that the generated 3D face shapes are far from the distribution of 3D faces in reality. We find that the above issues are caused by the training dataset's pose imbalance. In this paper, we present LPFF, a large-pose Flickr face dataset comprised of 19,590 high-quality real large-pose portrait images. We utilize our dataset to train a 2D face generator that can process large-pose face images, as well as a 3D-aware generator that can generate realistic human face geometry. To better validate our pose-conditional 3D-aware generators, we develop a new FID measure to evaluate the 3D-level performance. Through this novel FID measure and other experiments, we show that LPFF can help 2D face generators extend their latent space and better manipulate the large-pose data, and help 3D-aware face generators achieve better view consistency and more realistic 3D reconstruction results.
summary: The creation of 2D realistic facial images and 3D face shapes using generative networks has been a hot topic in recent years. Existing face generators exhibit exceptional performance on faces in small to medium poses (with respect to frontal faces) but struggle to produce realistic results for large poses. The distorted rendering results on large poses in 3D-aware generators further show that the generated 3D face shapes are far from the distribution of 3D faces in reality. We find that the above issues are caused by the training dataset's pose imbalance. In this paper, we present LPFF, a large-pose Flickr face dataset comprised of 19,590 high-quality real large-pose portrait images. We utilize our dataset to train a 2D face generator that can process large-pose face images, as well as a 3D-aware generator that can generate realistic human face geometry. To better validate our pose-conditional 3D-aware generators, we develop a new FID measure to evaluate the 3D-level performance. Through this novel FID measure and other experiments, we show that LPFF can help 2D face generators extend their latent space and better manipulate the large-pose data, and help 3D-aware face generators achieve better view consistency and more realistic 3D reconstruction results.
abstract_zh: 使用生成网络创建 2D 逼真面部图像和 3D 面部形状一直是近年来的热门话题。现有的面部生成器在小到中等姿势（相对于正面）的面部上表现出卓越的性能，但很难为大姿势产生逼真的结果。 3D 感知生成器中大姿势的扭曲渲染结果进一步表明，生成的 3D 人脸形状与现实中 3D 人脸的分布相距甚远。我们发现上述问题是由训练数据集的姿态不平衡引起的。在本文中，我们提出了 LPFF，一个由 19,590 张高质量真实大姿势肖像图像组成的大姿势 Flickr 人脸数据集。我们利用我们的数据集来训练一个可以处理大姿势人脸图像的 2D 人脸生成器，以及一个可以生成真实人脸几何形状的 3D 感知生成器。为了更好地验证我们的姿势条件 3D 感知生成器，我们开发了一种新的 FID 测量来评估 3D 级性能。通过这种新颖的 FID 测量和其他实验，我们表明 LPFF 可以帮助 2D 人脸生成器扩展其潜在空间并更好地操纵大姿态数据，并帮助 3D 感知人脸生成器实现更好的视图一致性和更真实的 3D 重建结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LPFF数据集，用于解决大角度姿态下人脸生成器的性能不足问题。
AI.motivation: 现有生成模型在处理大角度姿态人脸时效果差，且3D生成器的重建结果不够真实，主要因训练数据姿态分布不均衡。
AI.method: 构建包含19,590张高质量大角度人脸图像的LPFF数据集，训练2D生成器和3D感知生成器，并开发新的FID指标评估3D生成效果。
AI.result: LPFF使2D生成器扩展潜在空间并提升大角度数据处理能力，3D生成器实现更一致的视角和更真实的重建结果。
AI.conclusion: LPFF有效缓解了大角度姿态生成难题，为相关研究提供了高质量数据支持和评估方法。

We find that the above issues are caused by the training dataset's pose imbalance. In this paper, we present LPFF, a large-pose Flickr face dataset comprised of 19,590 high-quality real large-pose portrait images. We utilize our dataset to train a 2D face generator that can process large-pose face images, as well as a 3D-aware generator that can generate realistic human face geometry. To better validate our pose-conditional 3D-aware generators, we develop a new FID measure to evaluate the 3D-level performance. Through this novel FID measure and other experiments, we show that LPFF can help 2D face generators extend their latent space and better manipulate the large-pose data, and help 3D-aware face generators achieve better view consistency and more realistic 3D reconstruction results. 使用生成网络创建 2D 逼真面部图像和 3D 面部形状一直是近年来的热门话题。现有的面部生成器在小到中等姿势（相对于正面）的面部上表现出卓越的性能，但很难为大姿势产生逼真的结果。 3D 感知生成器中大姿势的扭曲渲染结果进一步表明，生成的 3D 人脸形状与现实中 3D 人脸的分布相距甚远。我们发现上述问题是由训练数据集的姿态不平衡引起的。在本文中，我们提出了 LPFF，一个由 19,590 张高质量真实大姿势肖像图像组成的大姿势 Flickr 人脸数据集。我们利用我们的数据集来训练一个可以处理大姿势人脸图像的 2D 人脸生成器，以及一个可以生成真实人脸几何形状的 3D 感知生成器。为了更好地验证我们的姿势条件 3D 感知生成器，我们开发了一种新的 FID 测量来评估 3D 级性能。通过这种新颖的 FID 测量和其他实验，我们表明 LPFF 可以帮助 2D 人脸生成器扩展其潜在空间并更好地操纵大姿态数据，并帮助 3D 感知人脸生成器实现更好的视图一致性和更真实的 3D 重建结果。 本文提出LPFF数据集，用于解决大角度姿态下人脸生成器的性能不足问题。 Yiqian Wu,Jing Zhang,Hongbo Fu,Xiaogang Jin cs.CV

authors_text: Muhammad Akhtar Munir,Muhammad Haris Khan,Salman Khan,Fahad Shahbaz Khan
title: Bridging Precision and Confidence: A Train-Time Loss for Calibrating Object Detection
title_zh: 连接精度和置信度：校准目标检测的训练时间损失
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have enabled astounding progress in several vision-based problems. Despite showing high predictive accuracy, recently, several works have revealed that they tend to provide overconfident predictions and thus are poorly calibrated. The majority of the works addressing the miscalibration of DNNs fall under the scope of classification and consider only in-domain predictions. However, there is little to no progress in studying the calibration of DNN-based object detection models, which are central to many vision-based safety-critical applications. In this paper, inspired by the train-time calibration methods, we propose a novel auxiliary loss formulation that explicitly aims to align the class confidence of bounding boxes with the accurateness of predictions (i.e. precision). Since the original formulation of our loss depends on the counts of true positives and false positives in a minibatch, we develop a differentiable proxy of our loss that can be used during training with other application-specific loss functions. We perform extensive experiments on challenging in-domain and out-domain scenarios with six benchmark datasets including MS-COCO, Cityscapes, Sim10k, and BDD100k. Our results reveal that our train-time loss surpasses strong calibration baselines in reducing calibration error for both in and out-domain scenarios. Our source code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/akhtarvision/bpc_calibration

Despite showing high predictive accuracy, recently, several works have revealed that they tend to provide overconfident predictions and thus are poorly calibrated. The majority of the works addressing the miscalibration of DNNs fall under the scope of classification and consider only in-domain predictions. However, there is little to no progress in studying the calibration of DNN-based object detection models, which are central to many vision-based safety-critical applications. In this paper, inspired by the train-time calibration methods, we propose a novel auxiliary loss formulation that explicitly aims to align the class confidence of bounding boxes with the accurateness of predictions (i.e. precision). Since the original formulation of our loss depends on the counts of true positives and false positives in a minibatch, we develop a differentiable proxy of our loss that can be used during training with other application-specific loss functions. We perform extensive experiments on challenging in-domain and out-domain scenarios with six benchmark datasets including MS-COCO, Cityscapes, Sim10k, and BDD100k. Our results reveal that our train-time loss surpasses strong calibration baselines in reducing calibration error for both in and out-domain scenarios. Our source code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/akhtarvision/bpc_calibration
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络 (DNN) 在一些基于视觉的问题上取得了惊人的进展。尽管显示出很高的预测准确性，但最近的几项研究表明，它们往往会提供过于自信的预测，因此校准不佳。大多数解决 DNN 校准错误的工作都属于分类范围，并且仅考虑域内预测。然而，基于 DNN 的目标检测模型的校准研究几乎没有进展，而该模型对于许多基于视觉的安全关键应用来说至关重要。在本文中，受训练时间校准方法的启发，我们提出了一种新颖的辅助损失公式，其明确目标是使边界框的类置信度与预测的准确性（即精度）保持一致。由于我们损失的原始公式取决于小批量中真阳性和假阳性的计数，因此我们开发了一个可微分的损失代理，可以在训练期间与其他特定于应用程序的损失函数一起使用。我们使用包括 MS-COCO、Cityscapes、Sim10k 和 BDD100k 在内的六个基准数据集，对具有挑战性的域内和域外场景进行了广泛的实验。我们的结果表明，我们的训练时间损失在减少域内和域外场景的校准误差方面超过了强大的校准基线。我们的源代码和预训练模型可在 https://github.com/akhtarvision/bpc_calibration 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种训练时损失函数，用于校准目标检测模型的置信度，使其更符合预测精度。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络在目标检测中存在过度自信的问题，现有校准方法多针对分类任务且仅适用于领域内数据，缺乏对检测模型的有效校准研究。
AI.method: 设计一种辅助损失函数，通过 minibatch 中真实正例和假正例的数量，显式对齐边界框的类别置信度与预测精度，并开发可微分代理损失以兼容其他任务损失函数。
AI.result: 在 MS-COCO、Cityscapes 等六个基准数据集的领域内和领域外场景中，该损失函数显著优于现有校准基线方法，降低校准误差。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了目标检测模型的校准性能，为安全关键型视觉应用提供了更可靠的预测置信度评估。

authors_text: Sukmin Yun,Seong Hyeon Park,Paul Hongsuck Seo,Jinwoo Shin
title: IFSeg: Image-free Semantic Segmentation via Vision-Language Model
title_zh: IFSeg：通过视觉语言模型进行无图像语义分割
abstract: Vision-language (VL) pre-training has recently gained much attention for its transferability and flexibility in novel concepts (e.g., cross-modality transfer) across various visual tasks. However, VL-driven segmentation has been under-explored, and the existing approaches still have the burden of acquiring additional training images or even segmentation annotations to adapt a VL model to downstream segmentation tasks. In this paper, we introduce a novel image-free segmentation task where the goal is to perform semantic segmentation given only a set of the target semantic categories, but without any task-specific images and annotations. To tackle this challenging task, our proposed method, coined IFSeg, generates VL-driven artificial image-segmentation pairs and updates a pre-trained VL model to a segmentation task. We construct this artificial training data by creating a 2D map of random semantic categories and another map of their corresponding word tokens. Given that a pre-trained VL model projects visual and text tokens into a common space where tokens that share the semantics are located closely, this artificially generated word map can replace the real image inputs for such a VL model. Through an extensive set of experiments, our model not only establishes an effective baseline for this novel task but also demonstrates strong performances compared to existing methods that rely on stronger supervision, such as task-specific images and segmentation masks. Code is available at https://github.com/alinlab/ifseg.
summary: Vision-language (VL) pre-training has recently gained much attention for its transferability and flexibility in novel concepts (e.g., cross-modality transfer) across various visual tasks. However, VL-driven segmentation has been under-explored, and the existing approaches still have the burden of acquiring additional training images or even segmentation annotations to adapt a VL model to downstream segmentation tasks. In this paper, we introduce a novel image-free segmentation task where the goal is to perform semantic segmentation given only a set of the target semantic categories, but without any task-specific images and annotations. To tackle this challenging task, our proposed method, coined IFSeg, generates VL-driven artificial image-segmentation pairs and updates a pre-trained VL model to a segmentation task. We construct this artificial training data by creating a 2D map of random semantic categories and another map of their corresponding word tokens. Given that a pre-trained VL model projects visual and text tokens into a common space where tokens that share the semantics are located closely, this artificially generated word map can replace the real image inputs for such a VL model. Through an extensive set of experiments, our model not only establishes an effective baseline for this novel task but also demonstrates strong performances compared to existing methods that rely on stronger supervision, such as task-specific images and segmentation masks. Code is available at https://github.com/alinlab/ifseg.
abstract_zh: 视觉语言（VL）预训练最近因其在各种视觉任务中新概念（例如跨模态迁移）的可迁移性和灵活性而受到广泛关注。然而，VL 驱动的分割尚未得到充分探索，现有方法仍然需要获取额外的训练图像甚至分割注释，以使 VL 模型适应下游分割任务。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的无图像分割任务，其目标是仅在给定一组目标语义类别的情况下执行语义分割，但没有任何特定于任务的图像和注释。为了解决这一具有挑战性的任务，我们提出的方法，即 IFSeg，生成 VL 驱动的人工图像分割对，并将预训练的 VL 模型更新为分割任务。我们通过创建随机语义类别的 2D 地图和其相应单词标记的另一个地图来构建此人工训练数据。鉴于预先训练的 VL 模型将视觉和文本标记投影到共享语义的标记位置紧密的公共空间中，这种人工生成的词图可以替代此类 VL 模型的真实图像输入。通过大量的实验，我们的模型不仅为这项新任务建立了有效的基线，而且与依赖更强监督的现有方法（例如特定于任务的图像和分割掩模）相比，还表现出了强大的性能。代码可在 https://github.com/alinlab/ifseg 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需图像的语义分割任务IFSeg，仅通过目标语义类别进行分割，无需任务特定图像和标注。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉-语言模型驱动的分割方法仍需依赖额外训练图像或分割标注，限制了其灵活性和适用性。
AI.method: 通过生成视觉-语言模型驱动的合成图像-分割对，利用预训练模型将随机语义类别的2D地图与对应文本标记映射到共同语义空间进行训练。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在无需任务特定图像和分割掩码的情况下，相比现有方法仍取得强竞争力的分割性能。
AI.conclusion: 证明了预训练视觉-语言模型可通过合成数据适配分割任务，为零样本分割和弱监督分割提供了新思路。

Given that a pre-trained VL model projects visual and text tokens into a common space where tokens that share the semantics are located closely, this artificially generated word map can replace the real image inputs for such a VL model. Through an extensive set of experiments, our model not only establishes an effective baseline for this novel task but also demonstrates strong performances compared to existing methods that rely on stronger supervision, such as task-specific images and segmentation masks. Code is available at https://github.com/alinlab/ifseg. 视觉语言（VL）预训练最近因其在各种视觉任务中新概念（例如跨模态迁移）的可迁移性和灵活性而受到广泛关注。然而，VL 驱动的分割尚未得到充分探索，现有方法仍然需要获取额外的训练图像甚至分割注释，以使 VL 模型适应下游分割任务。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的无图像分割任务，其目标是仅在给定一组目标语义类别的情况下执行语义分割，但没有任何特定于任务的图像和注释。为了解决这一具有挑战性的任务，我们提出的方法，即 IFSeg，生成 VL 驱动的人工图像分割对，并将预训练的 VL 模型更新为分割任务。我们通过创建随机语义类别的 2D 地图和其相应单词标记的另一个地图来构建此人工训练数据。鉴于预先训练的 VL 模型将视觉和文本标记投影到共享语义的标记位置紧密的公共空间中，这种人工生成的词图可以替代此类 VL 模型的真实图像输入。通过大量的实验，我们的模型不仅为这项新任务建立了有效的基线，而且与依赖更强监督的现有方法（例如特定于任务的图像和分割掩模）相比，还表现出了强大的性能。代码可在 https://github.com/alinlab/ifseg 获取。 本文提出一种无需图像的语义分割任务IFSeg，仅通过目标语义类别进行分割，无需任务特定图像和标注。 Sukmin Yun,Seong Hyeon Park,Paul Hongsuck Seo,Jinwoo Shin cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Minghan Li,Shuai Li,Wangmeng Xiang,Lei Zhang
title: MDQE: Mining Discriminative Query Embeddings to Segment Occluded Instances on Challenging Videos
title_zh: MDQE：挖掘判别性查询嵌入来分割具有挑战性的视频上的被遮挡实例
comment: ef:The IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition 2023
comments: ef:The IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition 2023
abstract: While impressive progress has been achieved, video instance segmentation (VIS) methods with per-clip input often fail on challenging videos with occluded objects and crowded scenes. This is mainly because instance queries in these methods cannot encode well the discriminative embeddings of instances, making the query-based segmenter difficult to distinguish those `hard' instances. To address these issues, we propose to mine discriminative query embeddings (MDQE) to segment occluded instances on challenging videos. First, we initialize the positional embeddings and content features of object queries by considering their spatial contextual information and the inter-frame object motion. Second, we propose an inter-instance mask repulsion loss to distance each instance from its nearby non-target instances. The proposed MDQE is the first VIS method with per-clip input that achieves state-of-the-art results on challenging videos and competitive performance on simple videos. In specific, MDQE with ResNet50 achieves 33.0\% and 44.5\% mask AP on OVIS and YouTube-VIS 2021, respectively. Code of MDQE can be found at \url{https://github.com/MinghanLi/MDQE_CVPR2023}.
summary: While impressive progress has been achieved, video instance segmentation (VIS) methods with per-clip input often fail on challenging videos with occluded objects and crowded scenes. This is mainly because instance queries in these methods cannot encode well the discriminative embeddings of instances, making the query-based segmenter difficult to distinguish those `hard' instances. To address these issues, we propose to mine discriminative query embeddings (MDQE) to segment occluded instances on challenging videos. First, we initialize the positional embeddings and content features of object queries by considering their spatial contextual information and the inter-frame object motion. Second, we propose an inter-instance mask repulsion loss to distance each instance from its nearby non-target instances. The proposed MDQE is the first VIS method with per-clip input that achieves state-of-the-art results on challenging videos and competitive performance on simple videos. In specific, MDQE with ResNet50 achieves 33.0\% and 44.5\% mask AP on OVIS and YouTube-VIS 2021, respectively. Code of MDQE can be found at \url{https://github.com/MinghanLi/MDQE_CVPR2023}.
abstract_zh: 虽然已经取得了令人印象深刻的进展，但采用每个剪辑输入的视频实例分割 (VIS) 方法在处理具有遮挡对象和拥挤场景的视频时通常会失败。这主要是因为这些方法中的实例查询无法很好地编码实例的判别性嵌入，使得基于查询的分段器难以区分那些“硬”实例。为了解决这些问题，我们建议挖掘判别性查询嵌入（MDQE）来分割具有挑战性的视频上被遮挡的实例。首先，我们通过考虑对象查询的空间上下文信息和帧间对象运动来初始化对象查询的位置嵌入和内容特征。其次，我们提出了实例间掩模排斥损失，以将每个实例与其附近的非目标实例分开。所提出的 MDQE 是第一个具有每个剪辑输入的 VIS 方法，它在具有挑战性的视频上取得了最先进的结果，在简单视频上取得了有竞争力的性能。具体来说，使用 ResNet50 的 MDQE 在 OVIS 和 YouTube-VIS 2021 上分别实现了 33.0% 和 44.5% 的掩模 AP。 MDQE的代码可以在\url{https://github.com/MinghanLi/MDQE_CVPR2023}找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MDQE方法，通过挖掘区分性查询嵌入来解决复杂视频中遮挡实例分割的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有视频实例分割方法在处理遮挡和拥挤场景时表现不佳，因实例查询无法有效编码区分性嵌入，导致难以区分困难实例。
AI.method: 首先通过空间上下文信息和帧间运动初始化查询的位置嵌入和内容特征；其次提出实例间掩码排斥损失，使目标实例远离非目标实例。
AI.result: MDQE在OVIS和YouTube-VIS 2021数据集上分别达到33.0%和44.5%的mask AP，且代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: MDQE是首个在复杂视频中达到SOTA的per-clip输入VIS方法，在简单视频中也表现优异。

authors_text: Zhen Zhang,Masaki Takeda,Makoto Iwata
title: Multi-pooling 3D Convolutional Neural Network for fMRI Classification of Visual Brain States
title_zh: 用于视觉大脑状态 fMRI 分类的多池 3D 卷积神经网络
comment: mber:2023 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI)
comments: mber:2023 IEEE Conference on Artificial Intelligence (CAI)
abstract: Neural decoding of visual object classification via functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) data is challenging and is vital to understand underlying brain mechanisms. This paper proposed a multi-pooling 3D convolutional neural network (MP3DCNN) to improve fMRI classification accuracy. MP3DCNN is mainly composed of a three-layer 3DCNN, where the first and second layers of 3D convolutions each have a branch of pooling connection. The results showed that this model can improve the classification accuracy for categorical (face vs. object), face sub-categorical (male face vs. female face), and object sub-categorical (natural object vs. artificial object) classifications from 1.684% to 14.918% over the previous study in decoding brain mechanisms.
summary: Neural decoding of visual object classification via functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) data is challenging and is vital to understand underlying brain mechanisms. This paper proposed a multi-pooling 3D convolutional neural network (MP3DCNN) to improve fMRI classification accuracy. MP3DCNN is mainly composed of a three-layer 3DCNN, where the first and second layers of 3D convolutions each have a branch of pooling connection. The results showed that this model can improve the classification accuracy for categorical (face vs. object), face sub-categorical (male face vs. female face), and object sub-categorical (natural object vs. artificial object) classifications from 1.684% to 14.918% over the previous study in decoding brain mechanisms.
abstract_zh: 通过功能磁共振成像 (fMRI) 数据对视觉对象分类进行神经解码具有挑战性，但对于理解潜在的大脑机制至关重要。本文提出了一种多池化 3D 卷积神经网络（MP3DCNN）来提高 fMRI 分类精度。 MP3DCNN主要由三层3DCNN组成，其中第一层和第二层3D卷积各有一个池化连接分支。结果表明，该模型可以将类别（人脸与物体）、人脸子类别（男性人脸与女性人脸）和物体子类别（自然物体与人造物体）分类的分类准确率比之前解码大脑机制的研究从1.684％提高到14.918％。

AI.tldr: 本文提出多池化3D卷积神经网络（MP3DCNN）以提升fMRI数据分类视觉脑状态的准确率。
AI.motivation: 现有fMRI视觉分类方法准确率低，需改进模型结构以更精确解码大脑视觉机制。
AI.method: 构建三层3D卷积神经网络，前两层分别引入池化分支实现多尺度特征提取。
AI.result: 分类准确率从1.684%提升至14.918%，在类别及子类别任务中均表现显著优化。
AI.conclusion: MP3DCNN通过多池化结构有效增强特征表达，验证了模型在脑状态分类中的优越性。

authors_text: Shanghua Gao,Pan Zhou,Ming-Ming Cheng,Shuicheng Yan
title: MDTv2: Masked Diffusion Transformer is a Strong Image Synthesizer
title_zh: MDTv2：掩模扩散变压器是一个强大的图像合成器
comment: Extension of ICCV 2023 work, source code: https://github.com/sail-sg/MDT
comments: Extension of ICCV 2023 work, source code: https://github.com/sail-sg/MDT
abstract: Despite its success in image synthesis, we observe that diffusion probabilistic models (DPMs) often lack contextual reasoning ability to learn the relations among object parts in an image, leading to a slow learning process. To solve this issue, we propose a Masked Diffusion Transformer (MDT) that introduces a mask latent modeling scheme to explicitly enhance the DPMs' ability to contextual relation learning among object semantic parts in an image. During training, MDT operates in the latent space to mask certain tokens. Then, an asymmetric diffusion transformer is designed to predict masked tokens from unmasked ones while maintaining the diffusion generation process. Our MDT can reconstruct the full information of an image from its incomplete contextual input, thus enabling it to learn the associated relations among image tokens. We further improve MDT with a more efficient macro network structure and training strategy, named MDTv2. Experimental results show that MDTv2 achieves superior image synthesis performance, e.g., a new SOTA FID score of 1.58 on the ImageNet dataset, and has more than 10x faster learning speed than the previous SOTA DiT. The source code is released at https://github.com/sail-sg/MDT.
summary: Despite its success in image synthesis, we observe that diffusion probabilistic models (DPMs) often lack contextual reasoning ability to learn the relations among object parts in an image, leading to a slow learning process. To solve this issue, we propose a Masked Diffusion Transformer (MDT) that introduces a mask latent modeling scheme to explicitly enhance the DPMs' ability to contextual relation learning among object semantic parts in an image. During training, MDT operates in the latent space to mask certain tokens. Then, an asymmetric diffusion transformer is designed to predict masked tokens from unmasked ones while maintaining the diffusion generation process. Our MDT can reconstruct the full information of an image from its incomplete contextual input, thus enabling it to learn the associated relations among image tokens. We further improve MDT with a more efficient macro network structure and training strategy, named MDTv2. Experimental results show that MDTv2 achieves superior image synthesis performance, e.g., a new SOTA FID score of 1.58 on the ImageNet dataset, and has more than 10x faster learning speed than the previous SOTA DiT. The source code is released at https://github.com/sail-sg/MDT.
abstract_zh: 尽管在图像合成方面取得了成功，但我们观察到扩散概率模型（DPM）通常缺乏上下文推理能力来学习图像中对象部分之间的关​​系，从而导致学习过程缓慢。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种掩码扩散变换器（MDT），它引入了掩码潜在建模方案，以显式增强 DPM 进行图像中对象语义部分之间的上下文关系学习的能力。在训练期间，MDT 在潜在空间中运行以掩盖某些标记。然后，设计一个不对称扩散变压器来从未屏蔽的令牌中预测屏蔽的令牌，同时保持扩散生成过程。我们的 MDT 可以从不完整的上下文输入中重建图像的完整信息，从而使其能够学习图像标记之间的关联关系。我们通过更高效的宏观网络结构和训练策略进一步改进MDT，命名为MDTv2。实验结果表明，MDTv2 实现了卓越的图像合成性能，例如，在 ImageNet 数据集上新的 SOTA FID 得分为 1.58，并且学习速度比之前的 SOTA DiT 快 10 倍以上。源代码发布于 https://github.com/sail-sg/MDT。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MDTv2模型，通过改进扩散概率模型的上下文学习能力，提升图像生成效果。
AI.motivation: 现有扩散模型在学习图像中物体部件间关系时缺乏上下文推理能力，导致训练效率低下。
AI.method: 设计Masked Diffusion Transformer（MDT），在潜在空间中通过掩码机制增强部件关系学习，并优化为更高效的MDTv2结构与训练策略。
AI.result: 在ImageNet数据集上取得1.58的SOTA FID分数，训练速度较DiT模型提升10倍以上。
AI.conclusion: MDTv2通过上下文感知的掩码学习机制，显著提升了图像生成质量与训练效率。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出MDTv2模型，通过改进扩散概率模型的上下文学习能力，提升图像生成效果。
ai_content.motivation: 现有扩散模型在学习图像中物体部件间关系时缺乏上下文推理能力，导致训练效率低下。
ai_content.method: 设计Masked Diffusion Transformer（MDT），在潜在空间中通过掩码机制增强部件关系学习，并优化为更高效的MDTv2结构与训练策略。
ai_content.result: 在ImageNet数据集上取得1.58的SOTA FID分数，训练速度较DiT模型提升10倍以上。
ai_content.conclusion: MDTv2通过上下文感知的掩码学习机制，显著提升了图像生成质量与训练效率。

search_text: MDTv2: Masked Diffusion Transformer is a Strong Image Synthesizer MDTv2：掩模扩散变压器是一个强大的图像合成器 Despite its success in image synthesis, we observe that diffusion probabilistic models (DPMs) often lack contextual reasoning ability to learn the relations among object parts in an image, leading to a slow learning process. To solve this issue, we propose a Masked Diffusion Transformer (MDT) that introduces a mask latent modeling scheme to explicitly enhance the DPMs' ability to contextual relation learning among object semantic parts in an image. During training, MDT operates in the latent space to mask certain tokens. Then, an asymmetric diffusion transformer is designed to predict masked tokens from unmasked ones while maintaining the diffusion generation process. Our MDT can reconstruct the full information of an image from its incomplete contextual input, thus enabling it to learn the associated relations among image tokens. We further improve MDT with a more efficient macro network structure and training strategy, named MDTv2. Experimental results show that MDTv2 achieves superior image synthesis performance, e.g., a new SOTA FID score of 1.58 on the ImageNet dataset, and has more than 10x faster learning speed than the previous SOTA DiT. The source code is released at https://github.com/sail-sg/MDT. 尽管在图像合成方面取得了成功，但我们观察到扩散概率模型（DPM）通常缺乏上下文推理能力来学习图像中对象部分之间的关​​系，从而导致学习过程缓慢。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种掩码扩散变换器（MDT），它引入了掩码潜在建模方案，以显式增强 DPM 进行图像中对象语义部分之间的上下文关系学习的能力。在训练期间，MDT 在潜在空间中运行以掩盖某些标记。然后，设计一个不对称扩散变压器来从未屏蔽的令牌中预测屏蔽的令牌，同时保持扩散生成过程。我们的 MDT 可以从不完整的上下文输入中重建图像的完整信息，从而使其能够学习图像标记之间的关联关系。我们通过更高效的宏观网络结构和训练策略进一步改进MDT，命名为MDTv2。实验结果表明，MDTv2 实现了卓越的图像合成性能，例如，在 ImageNet 数据集上新的 SOTA FID 得分为 1.58，并且学习速度比之前的 SOTA DiT 快 10 倍以上。源代码发布于 https://github.com/sail-sg/MDT。 本文提出MDTv2模型，通过改进扩散概率模型的上下文学习能力，提升图像生成效果。 Shanghua Gao,Pan Zhou,Ming-Ming Cheng,Shuicheng Yan cs.CV

authors_text: Hwanjun Song,Jihwan Bang
title: Prompt-Guided Transformers for End-to-End Open-Vocabulary Object Detection
title_zh: 用于端到端开放词汇目标检测的提示引导变压器
abstract: Prompt-OVD is an efficient and effective framework for open-vocabulary object detection that utilizes class embeddings from CLIP as prompts, guiding the Transformer decoder to detect objects in both base and novel classes. Additionally, our novel RoI-based masked attention and RoI pruning techniques help leverage the zero-shot classification ability of the Vision Transformer-based CLIP, resulting in improved detection performance at minimal computational cost. Our experiments on the OV-COCO and OVLVIS datasets demonstrate that Prompt-OVD achieves an impressive 21.2 times faster inference speed than the first end-to-end open-vocabulary detection method (OV-DETR), while also achieving higher APs than four two-stage-based methods operating within similar inference time ranges. Code will be made available soon.
summary: Prompt-OVD is an efficient and effective framework for open-vocabulary object detection that utilizes class embeddings from CLIP as prompts, guiding the Transformer decoder to detect objects in both base and novel classes. Additionally, our novel RoI-based masked attention and RoI pruning techniques help leverage the zero-shot classification ability of the Vision Transformer-based CLIP, resulting in improved detection performance at minimal computational cost. Our experiments on the OV-COCO and OVLVIS datasets demonstrate that Prompt-OVD achieves an impressive 21.2 times faster inference speed than the first end-to-end open-vocabulary detection method (OV-DETR), while also achieving higher APs than four two-stage-based methods operating within similar inference time ranges. Code will be made available soon.
abstract_zh: Prompt-OVD 是一种高效且有效的开放词汇对象检测框架，它利用 CLIP 中的类嵌入作为提示，指导 Transformer 解码器检测基类和新类中的对象。此外，我们新颖的基于 RoI 的掩模注意力和 RoI 修剪技术有助于利用基于 Vision Transformer 的 CLIP 的零样本分类能力，从而以最小的计算成本提高检测性能。我们在 OV-COCO 和 OVLVIS 数据集上的实验表明，Prompt-OVD 的推理速度比第一个端到端开放词汇检测方法 (OV-DETR) 快了 21.2 倍，同时还比在相似推理时间范围内运行的四种基于两阶段的方法实现了更高的 AP。代码将很快提供。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Prompt-OVD框架，利用CLIP的类别嵌入作为提示，结合Transformer解码器实现高效的开放词汇对象检测。
AI.motivation: 现有开放词汇检测方法计算成本高或性能不足，需更高效利用CLIP的零样本分类能力以提升检测效率与准确率。
AI.method: 通过RoI掩码注意力机制和RoI剪枝技术，将CLIP的类别嵌入作为提示引导Transformer解码器，实现端到端检测。
AI.result: 在OV-COCO和OVLVIS数据集上，Prompt-OVD比OV-DETR快21.2倍，且在相同推理时间内AP值优于四个两阶段方法。
AI.conclusion: Prompt-OVD在保持高检测精度的同时显著提升推理速度，为开放词汇检测提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Zikun Zhou,Kaige Mao,Wenjie Pei,Hongpeng Wang,Yaowei Wang,Zhenyu He
title: Reliability-Hierarchical Memory Network for Scribble-Supervised Video Object Segmentation
title_zh: 用于涂鸦监督视频对象分割的可靠性分层内存网络
abstract: This paper aims to solve the video object segmentation (VOS) task in a scribble-supervised manner, in which VOS models are not only trained by the sparse scribble annotations but also initialized with the sparse target scribbles for inference. Thus, the annotation burdens for both training and initialization can be substantially lightened. The difficulties of scribble-supervised VOS lie in two aspects. On the one hand, it requires the powerful ability to learn from the sparse scribble annotations during training. On the other hand, it demands strong reasoning capability during inference given only a sparse initial target scribble. In this work, we propose a Reliability-Hierarchical Memory Network (RHMNet) to predict the target mask in a step-wise expanding strategy w.r.t. the memory reliability level. To be specific, RHMNet first only uses the memory in the high-reliability level to locate the region with high reliability belonging to the target, which is highly similar to the initial target scribble. Then it expands the located high-reliability region to the entire target conditioned on the region itself and the memories in all reliability levels. Besides, we propose a scribble-supervised learning mechanism to facilitate the learning of our model to predict dense results. It mines the pixel-level relation within the single frame and the frame-level relation within the sequence to take full advantage of the scribble annotations in sequence training samples. The favorable performance on two popular benchmarks demonstrates that our method is promising.
summary: This paper aims to solve the video object segmentation (VOS) task in a scribble-supervised manner, in which VOS models are not only trained by the sparse scribble annotations but also initialized with the sparse target scribbles for inference. Thus, the annotation burdens for both training and initialization can be substantially lightened. The difficulties of scribble-supervised VOS lie in two aspects. On the one hand, it requires the powerful ability to learn from the sparse scribble annotations during training. On the other hand, it demands strong reasoning capability during inference given only a sparse initial target scribble. In this work, we propose a Reliability-Hierarchical Memory Network (RHMNet) to predict the target mask in a step-wise expanding strategy w.r.t. the memory reliability level. To be specific, RHMNet first only uses the memory in the high-reliability level to locate the region with high reliability belonging to the target, which is highly similar to the initial target scribble. Then it expands the located high-reliability region to the entire target conditioned on the region itself and the memories in all reliability levels. Besides, we propose a scribble-supervised learning mechanism to facilitate the learning of our model to predict dense results. It mines the pixel-level relation within the single frame and the frame-level relation within the sequence to take full advantage of the scribble annotations in sequence training samples. The favorable performance on two popular benchmarks demonstrates that our method is promising.
abstract_zh: 本文旨在以涂鸦监督的方式解决视频对象分割（VOS）任务，其中VOS模型不仅通过稀疏涂鸦注释进行训练，而且还利用稀疏目标涂鸦进行初始化以进行推理。因此，可以大大减轻训练和初始化的注释负担。涂鸦监督 VOS 的困难在于两个方面。一方面，它需要在训练过程中从稀疏的涂鸦注释中学习强大的能力。另一方面，在仅给出稀疏的初始目标涂鸦的情况下，它在推理过程中要求强大的推理能力。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种可靠性分层记忆网络（RHMNet）来以逐步扩展策略预测目标掩码。内存可靠性级别。具体来说，RHMNet首先只使用高可靠性级别的内存来定位属于目标的高可靠性区域，这与初始目标涂鸦高度相似。然后，以该区域本身和所有可靠性级别的存储器为条件，将所定位的高可靠性区域扩展到整个目标。此外，我们提出了一种涂鸦监督学习机制，以促进我们的模型的学习来预测密集结果。它挖掘单帧内的像素级关系和序列内的帧级关系，以充分利用序列训练样本中的涂鸦注释。两个流行基准测试的良好表现表明我们的方法很有前途。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于可靠性分层记忆网络（RHMNet）的草图监督视频目标分割方法，旨在通过稀疏草图标注减轻标注负担。
AI.motivation: 草图监督的视频目标分割面临训练时稀疏标注学习困难及推理时仅依赖稀疏初始草图的推理能力不足的双重挑战。
AI.method: RHMNet采用分步扩展策略，先利用高可靠性记忆定位与初始草图相似的高可靠区域，再结合全可靠性记忆扩展至完整目标；同时设计草图监督机制，挖掘像素级与帧级关系以提升模型学习效率。
AI.result: 在两个主流基准测试中表现优异，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: RHMNet通过可靠性分层记忆机制和草图监督学习，有效解决了草图监督下的视频目标分割问题，具有应用潜力。

The favorable performance on two popular benchmarks demonstrates that our method is promising. 本文旨在以涂鸦监督的方式解决视频对象分割（VOS）任务，其中VOS模型不仅通过稀疏涂鸦注释进行训练，而且还利用稀疏目标涂鸦进行初始化以进行推理。因此，可以大大减轻训练和初始化的注释负担。涂鸦监督 VOS 的困难在于两个方面。一方面，它需要在训练过程中从稀疏的涂鸦注释中学习强大的能力。另一方面，在仅给出稀疏的初始目标涂鸦的情况下，它在推理过程中要求强大的推理能力。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种可靠性分层记忆网络（RHMNet）来以逐步扩展策略预测目标掩码。内存可靠性级别。具体来说，RHMNet首先只使用高可靠性级别的内存来定位属于目标的高可靠性区域，这与初始目标涂鸦高度相似。然后，以该区域本身和所有可靠性级别的存储器为条件，将所定位的高可靠性区域扩展到整个目标。此外，我们提出了一种涂鸦监督学习机制，以促进我们的模型的学习来预测密集结果。它挖掘单帧内的像素级关系和序列内的帧级关系，以充分利用序列训练样本中的涂鸦注释。两个流行基准测试的良好表现表明我们的方法很有前途。 本文提出一种基于可靠性分层记忆网络（RHMNet）的草图监督视频目标分割方法，旨在通过稀疏草图标注减轻标注负担。 Zikun Zhou,Kaige Mao,Wenjie Pei,Hongpeng Wang,Yaowei Wang,Zhenyu He cs.CV

authors_text: Yichen Xie,Han Lu,Junchi Yan,Xiaokang Yang,Masayoshi Tomizuka,Wei Zhan
title: Active Finetuning: Exploiting Annotation Budget in the Pretraining-Finetuning Paradigm
title_zh: 主动微调：在预训练-微调范式中利用注释预算
abstract: Given the large-scale data and the high annotation cost, pretraining-finetuning becomes a popular paradigm in multiple computer vision tasks. Previous research has covered both the unsupervised pretraining and supervised finetuning in this paradigm, while little attention is paid to exploiting the annotation budget for finetuning. To fill in this gap, we formally define this new active finetuning task focusing on the selection of samples for annotation in the pretraining-finetuning paradigm. We propose a novel method called ActiveFT for active finetuning task to select a subset of data distributing similarly with the entire unlabeled pool and maintaining enough diversity by optimizing a parametric model in the continuous space. We prove that the Earth Mover's distance between the distributions of the selected subset and the entire data pool is also reduced in this process. Extensive experiments show the leading performance and high efficiency of ActiveFT superior to baselines on both image classification and semantic segmentation. Our code is released at https://github.com/yichen928/ActiveFT.
summary: Given the large-scale data and the high annotation cost, pretraining-finetuning becomes a popular paradigm in multiple computer vision tasks. Previous research has covered both the unsupervised pretraining and supervised finetuning in this paradigm, while little attention is paid to exploiting the annotation budget for finetuning. To fill in this gap, we formally define this new active finetuning task focusing on the selection of samples for annotation in the pretraining-finetuning paradigm. We propose a novel method called ActiveFT for active finetuning task to select a subset of data distributing similarly with the entire unlabeled pool and maintaining enough diversity by optimizing a parametric model in the continuous space. We prove that the Earth Mover's distance between the distributions of the selected subset and the entire data pool is also reduced in this process. Extensive experiments show the leading performance and high efficiency of ActiveFT superior to baselines on both image classification and semantic segmentation. Our code is released at https://github.com/yichen928/ActiveFT.
abstract_zh: 鉴于大规模数据和高标注成本，预训练-微调成为多种计算机视觉任务中的流行范例。先前的研究涵盖了该范式中的无监督预训练和监督微调，而很少关注利用注释预算进行微调。为了填补这一空白，我们正式定义了这个新的主动微调任务，重点关注在预训练微调范例中选择用于注释的样本。我们提出了一种称为 ActiveFT 的新颖方法，用于主动微调任务，以选择与整个未标记池分布相似的数据子集，并通过优化连续空间中的参数模型来保持足够的多样性。我们证明，在这个过程中，Earth Mover 所选子集的分布与整个数据池之间的距离也减小了。大量实验表明，ActiveFT 在图像分类和语义分割方面均优于基线，具有领先的性能和高效率。我们的代码发布在https://github.com/yichen928/ActiveFT。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为ActiveFT的主动微调方法，在预训练-微调范式中有效利用标注预算，通过选择具有代表性的样本进行标注以提高模型性能。
AI.motivation: 由于大规模数据和高标注成本，传统预训练-微调范式中对标注预算的利用不足，亟需一种有效的方法来优化微调阶段的样本选择，以提升模型性能并减少标注需求。
AI.method: 提出ActiveFT方法，通过在连续空间中优化参数模型，选择与整个未标记数据池分布相似且保持多样性的子集，从而降低所选子集与整体数据池之间的地球移动距离（EMD）。
AI.result: 在图像分类和语义分割任务中，ActiveFT相比基线方法表现出更优的性能和更高的效率。
AI.conclusion: ActiveFT有效解决了预训练-微调范式中的标注预算利用问题，为高效模型训练提供了新思路。

authors_text: Phuong D. Nguyen,Thinh D. Le,Duong Q. Nguyen,Thanh Q. Nguyen,Li-Wei Chou,H. Nguyen-Xuan
title: 3D Facial Imperfection Regeneration: Deep learning approach and 3D printing prototypes
title_zh: 3D面部缺陷再生：深度学习方法和3D打印原型
abstract: This study explores the potential of a fully convolutional mesh autoencoder model for regenerating 3D nature faces with the presence of imperfect areas. We utilize deep learning approaches in graph processing and analysis to investigate the capabilities model in recreating a filling part for facial scars. Our approach in dataset creation is able to generate a facial scar rationally in a virtual space that corresponds to the unique circumstances. Especially, we propose a new method which is named 3D Facial Imperfection Regeneration(3D-FaIR) for reproducing a complete face reconstruction based on the remaining features of the patient face. To further enhance the applicable capacity of the present research, we develop an improved outlier technique to separate the wounds of patients and provide appropriate wound cover models. Also, a Cir3D-FaIR dataset of imperfect faces and open codes was released at https://github.com/SIMOGroup/3DFaIR. Our findings demonstrate the potential of the proposed approach to help patients recover more quickly and safely through convenient techniques. We hope that this research can contribute to the development of new products and innovative solutions for facial scar regeneration.
summary: This study explores the potential of a fully convolutional mesh autoencoder model for regenerating 3D nature faces with the presence of imperfect areas. We utilize deep learning approaches in graph processing and analysis to investigate the capabilities model in recreating a filling part for facial scars. Our approach in dataset creation is able to generate a facial scar rationally in a virtual space that corresponds to the unique circumstances. Especially, we propose a new method which is named 3D Facial Imperfection Regeneration(3D-FaIR) for reproducing a complete face reconstruction based on the remaining features of the patient face. To further enhance the applicable capacity of the present research, we develop an improved outlier technique to separate the wounds of patients and provide appropriate wound cover models. Also, a Cir3D-FaIR dataset of imperfect faces and open codes was released at https://github.com/SIMOGroup/3DFaIR. Our findings demonstrate the potential of the proposed approach to help patients recover more quickly and safely through convenient techniques. We hope that this research can contribute to the development of new products and innovative solutions for facial scar regeneration.
abstract_zh: 本研究探讨了全卷积网格自动编码器模型在存在不完美区域的情况下重新生成 3D 自然面孔的潜力。我们利用图形处理和分析中的深度学习方法来研究重建面部疤痕填充部分的能力模型。我们创建数据集的方法能够在与独特情况相对应的虚拟空间中合理地生成面部疤痕。特别是，我们提出了一种名为 3D 面部不完美再生（3D-FaIR）的新方法，用于根据患者面部的剩余特征再现完整的面部重建。为了进一步增强本研究的应用能力，我们开发了一种改进的异常值技术来分离患者的伤口并提供合适的伤口覆盖模型。此外，不完美面孔和开放代码的 Cir3D-FaIR 数据集已在 https://github.com/SIMOGroup/3DFaIR 上发布。我们的研究结果证明了所提出的方法有潜力通过方便的技术帮助患者更快、更安全地康复。我们希望这项研究能够为面部疤痕再生的新产品和创新解决方案的开发做出贡献。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习和3D打印技术的面部瑕疵再生方法，通过全卷积网格自编码器模型修复面部疤痕并生成3D打印原型。
AI.motivation: 传统面部疤痕修复方法存在恢复周期长、效果有限等问题，需探索更高效且符合生理特征的数字化修复方案。
AI.method: 构建全卷积网格自编码器模型处理3D面部网格数据，提出3D-FaIR算法实现瑕疵区域再生，并开发异常点分离技术生成个性化修复模型。
AI.result: 成功生成符合面部解剖结构的疤痕修复方案，创建包含瑕疵面部数据的Cir3D-FaIR数据集，开源代码可复现实验结果。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为面部疤痕修复提供了自动化数字解决方案，未来可结合生物材料打印实现临床应用，推动个性化医疗发展。

authors_text: Kwonyoung Ryu,Soonmin Hwang,Jaesik Park
title: Instant Domain Augmentation for LiDAR Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 用于 LiDAR 语义分割的即时域增强
abstract: Despite the increasing popularity of LiDAR sensors, perception algorithms using 3D LiDAR data struggle with the 'sensor-bias problem'. Specifically, the performance of perception algorithms significantly drops when an unseen specification of LiDAR sensor is applied at test time due to the domain discrepancy. This paper presents a fast and flexible LiDAR augmentation method for the semantic segmentation task, called 'LiDomAug'. It aggregates raw LiDAR scans and creates a LiDAR scan of any configurations with the consideration of dynamic distortion and occlusion, resulting in instant domain augmentation. Our on-demand augmentation module runs at 330 FPS, so it can be seamlessly integrated into the data loader in the learning framework. In our experiments, learning-based approaches aided with the proposed LiDomAug are less affected by the sensor-bias issue and achieve new state-of-the-art domain adaptation performances on SemanticKITTI and nuScenes dataset without the use of the target domain data. We also present a sensor-agnostic model that faithfully works on the various LiDAR configurations.
summary: Despite the increasing popularity of LiDAR sensors, perception algorithms using 3D LiDAR data struggle with the 'sensor-bias problem'. Specifically, the performance of perception algorithms significantly drops when an unseen specification of LiDAR sensor is applied at test time due to the domain discrepancy. This paper presents a fast and flexible LiDAR augmentation method for the semantic segmentation task, called 'LiDomAug'. It aggregates raw LiDAR scans and creates a LiDAR scan of any configurations with the consideration of dynamic distortion and occlusion, resulting in instant domain augmentation. Our on-demand augmentation module runs at 330 FPS, so it can be seamlessly integrated into the data loader in the learning framework. In our experiments, learning-based approaches aided with the proposed LiDomAug are less affected by the sensor-bias issue and achieve new state-of-the-art domain adaptation performances on SemanticKITTI and nuScenes dataset without the use of the target domain data. We also present a sensor-agnostic model that faithfully works on the various LiDAR configurations.
abstract_zh: 尽管 LiDAR 传感器越来越受欢迎，但使用 3D LiDAR 数据的感知算法仍面临“传感器偏差问题”。具体来说，当在测试时应用不可见的 LiDAR 传感器规格时，由于域差异，感知算法的性能会显着下降。本文提出了一种用于语义分割任务的快速灵活的 LiDAR 增强方法，称为“LiDomAug”。它聚合原始 LiDAR 扫描并创建任何配置的 LiDAR 扫描，同时考虑动态失真和遮挡，从而实现即时域增强。我们的按需增强模块以 330 FPS 运行，因此它可以无缝集成到学习框架中的数据加载器中。在我们的实验中，采用所提出的 LiDomAug 辅助的基于学习的方法受传感器偏差问题的影响较小，并且在 SemanticKITTI 和 nuScenes 数据集上实现了新的最先进的域适应性能，而无需使用目标域数据。我们还提出了一个与传感器无关的模型，可以忠实地适用于各种激光雷达配置。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为LiDomAug的即时领域增强方法，用于解决LiDAR语义分割中的传感器偏差问题。
AI.motivation: 现有LiDAR感知算法在测试时遇到未见过的传感器规格时，因领域差异导致性能显著下降。
AI.method: 通过聚合原始LiDAR扫描数据，生成考虑动态失真和遮挡的任意配置扫描，实现快速领域增强模块（330 FPS）。
AI.result: 在SemanticKITTI和nuScenes数据集上，使用LiDomAug的方法在无目标域数据情况下达到新的领域自适应效果，并提出传感器无关模型。
AI.conclusion: LiDomAug有效缓解传感器偏差问题，提升模型跨领域适应性，且无需目标域数据即可实现高性能语义分割。

search_text: Instant Domain Augmentation for LiDAR Semantic Segmentation 用于 LiDAR 语义分割的即时域增强 Despite the increasing popularity of LiDAR sensors, perception algorithms using 3D LiDAR data struggle with the 'sensor-bias problem'. Specifically, the performance of perception algorithms significantly drops when an unseen specification of LiDAR sensor is applied at test time due to the domain discrepancy. This paper presents a fast and flexible LiDAR augmentation method for the semantic segmentation task, called 'LiDomAug'. It aggregates raw LiDAR scans and creates a LiDAR scan of any configurations with the consideration of dynamic distortion and occlusion, resulting in instant domain augmentation. Our on-demand augmentation module runs at 330 FPS, so it can be seamlessly integrated into the data loader in the learning framework. In our experiments, learning-based approaches aided with the proposed LiDomAug are less affected by the sensor-bias issue and achieve new state-of-the-art domain adaptation performances on SemanticKITTI and nuScenes dataset without the use of the target domain data. We also present a sensor-agnostic model that faithfully works on the various LiDAR configurations. 尽管 LiDAR 传感器越来越受欢迎，但使用 3D LiDAR 数据的感知算法仍面临“传感器偏差问题”。具体来说，当在测试时应用不可见的 LiDAR 传感器规格时，由于域差异，感知算法的性能会显着下降。本文提出了一种用于语义分割任务的快速灵活的 LiDAR 增强方法，称为“LiDomAug”。它聚合原始 LiDAR 扫描并创建任何配置的 LiDAR 扫描，同时考虑动态失真和遮挡，从而实现即时域增强。我们的按需增强模块以 330 FPS 运行，因此它可以无缝集成到学习框架中的数据加载器中。在我们的实验中，采用所提出的 LiDomAug 辅助的基于学习的方法受传感器偏差问题的影响较小，并且在 SemanticKITTI 和 nuScenes 数据集上实现了新的最先进的域适应性能，而无需使用目标域数据。我们还提出了一个与传感器无关的模型，可以忠实地适用于各种激光雷达配置。 本文提出一种名为LiDomAug的即时领域增强方法，用于解决LiDAR语义分割中的传感器偏差问题。 Kwonyoung Ryu,Soonmin Hwang,Jaesik Park cs.CV

authors_text: Chenchen Xu,Min Zhou,Tiezheng Ge,Yuning Jiang,Weiwei Xu
title: Unsupervised Domain Adaption with Pixel-level Discriminator for Image-aware Layout Generation
title_zh: 具有像素级鉴别器的无监督域适应用于图像感知布局生成
comment: 8 pages, 4 figures, 7 tables, accepted by CVPR2023
comments: 8 pages, 4 figures, 7 tables, accepted by CVPR2023
abstract: Layout is essential for graphic design and poster generation. Recently, applying deep learning models to generate layouts has attracted increasing attention. This paper focuses on using the GAN-based model conditioned on image contents to generate advertising poster graphic layouts, which requires an advertising poster layout dataset with paired product images and graphic layouts. However, the paired images and layouts in the existing dataset are collected by inpainting and annotating posters, respectively. There exists a domain gap between inpainted posters (source domain data) and clean product images (target domain data). Therefore, this paper combines unsupervised domain adaption techniques to design a GAN with a novel pixel-level discriminator (PD), called PDA-GAN, to generate graphic layouts according to image contents. The PD is connected to the shallow level feature map and computes the GAN loss for each input-image pixel. Both quantitative and qualitative evaluations demonstrate that PDA-GAN can achieve state-of-the-art performances and generate high-quality image-aware graphic layouts for advertising posters.
summary: Layout is essential for graphic design and poster generation. Recently, applying deep learning models to generate layouts has attracted increasing attention. This paper focuses on using the GAN-based model conditioned on image contents to generate advertising poster graphic layouts, which requires an advertising poster layout dataset with paired product images and graphic layouts. However, the paired images and layouts in the existing dataset are collected by inpainting and annotating posters, respectively. There exists a domain gap between inpainted posters (source domain data) and clean product images (target domain data). Therefore, this paper combines unsupervised domain adaption techniques to design a GAN with a novel pixel-level discriminator (PD), called PDA-GAN, to generate graphic layouts according to image contents. The PD is connected to the shallow level feature map and computes the GAN loss for each input-image pixel. Both quantitative and qualitative evaluations demonstrate that PDA-GAN can achieve state-of-the-art performances and generate high-quality image-aware graphic layouts for advertising posters.
abstract_zh: 布局对于图形设计和海报生成至关重要。最近，应用深度学习模型生成布局引起了越来越多的关注。本文重点介绍使用基于图像内容的 GAN 模型来生成广告海报图形布局，这需要一个包含配对产品图像和图形布局的广告海报布局数据集。然而，现有数据集中的配对图像和布局是分别通过修复和注释海报来收集的。修复的海报（源域数据）和干净的产品图像（目标域数据）之间存在域差距。因此，本文结合无监督域适应技术，设计了一种带有新型像素级鉴别器（PD）的 GAN，称为 PDA-GAN，根据图像内容生成图形布局。 PD 连接到浅层特征图并计算每个输入图像像素的 GAN 损失。定量和定性评估都表明 PDA-GAN 可以实现最先进的性能，并为广告海报生成高质量的图像感知图形布局。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于像素级判别器的无监督域适应方法PDA-GAN，用于根据图像内容生成广告海报的图形布局。
AI.motivation: 现有数据集存在修复后海报（源域）与干净产品图像（目标域）之间的域差距，导致布局生成效果受限，需通过域适应技术解决此问题。
AI.method: 设计包含像素级判别器（PD）的GAN模型，将PD连接至浅层特征图并为每个像素计算GAN损失，实现源域与目标域的特征对齐。
AI.result: 定量与定性评估表明，PDA-GAN在广告海报图像感知布局生成任务中达到最先进水平，生成高质量布局。
AI.conclusion: 像素级判别器有效缓解域差距问题，证明无监督域适应技术可提升图像内容驱动的布局生成效果。

authors_text: Hongyu Sun,Yongcai Wang,Xudong Cai,Xuewei Bai,Deying Li
title: ViPFormer: Efficient Vision-and-Pointcloud Transformer for Unsupervised Pointcloud Understanding
title_zh: ViPFarmer：用于无监督点云理解的高效视觉和点云转换器
comment: 9 pages, 4 figures, 7 tables; accepted by ICRA 2023
comments: 9 pages, 4 figures, 7 tables; accepted by ICRA 2023
abstract: Recently, a growing number of work design unsupervised paradigms for point cloud processing to alleviate the limitation of expensive manual annotation and poor transferability of supervised methods. Among them, CrossPoint follows the contrastive learning framework and exploits image and point cloud data for unsupervised point cloud understanding. Although the promising performance is presented, the unbalanced architecture makes it unnecessarily complex and inefficient. For example, the image branch in CrossPoint is $\sim$8.3x heavier than the point cloud branch leading to higher complexity and latency. To address this problem, in this paper, we propose a lightweight Vision-and-Pointcloud Transformer (ViPFormer) to unify image and point cloud processing in a single architecture. ViPFormer learns in an unsupervised manner by optimizing intra-modal and cross-modal contrastive objectives. Then the pretrained model is transferred to various downstream tasks, including 3D shape classification and semantic segmentation. Experiments on different datasets show ViPFormer surpasses previous state-of-the-art unsupervised methods with higher accuracy, lower model complexity and runtime latency. Finally, the effectiveness of each component in ViPFormer is validated by extensive ablation studies. The implementation of the proposed method is available at https://github.com/auniquesun/ViPFormer.
summary: Recently, a growing number of work design unsupervised paradigms for point cloud processing to alleviate the limitation of expensive manual annotation and poor transferability of supervised methods. Among them, CrossPoint follows the contrastive learning framework and exploits image and point cloud data for unsupervised point cloud understanding. Although the promising performance is presented, the unbalanced architecture makes it unnecessarily complex and inefficient. For example, the image branch in CrossPoint is $\sim$8.3x heavier than the point cloud branch leading to higher complexity and latency. To address this problem, in this paper, we propose a lightweight Vision-and-Pointcloud Transformer (ViPFormer) to unify image and point cloud processing in a single architecture. ViPFormer learns in an unsupervised manner by optimizing intra-modal and cross-modal contrastive objectives. Then the pretrained model is transferred to various downstream tasks, including 3D shape classification and semantic segmentation. Experiments on different datasets show ViPFormer surpasses previous state-of-the-art unsupervised methods with higher accuracy, lower model complexity and runtime latency. Finally, the effectiveness of each component in ViPFormer is validated by extensive ablation studies. The implementation of the proposed method is available at https://github.com/auniquesun/ViPFormer.
abstract_zh: 最近，越来越多的工作设计了点云处理的无监督范例，以减轻昂贵的手动注释和监督方法可移植性差的限制。其中，CrossPoint遵循对比学习框架，利用图像和点云数据进行无监督点云理解。尽管性能表现出色，但不平衡的架构使其变得不必要的复杂和低效。例如，CrossPoint 中的图像分支比点云分支重 $\sim$8.3 倍，导致更高的复杂性和延迟。为了解决这个问题，在本文中，我们提出了一种轻量级的视觉和点云转换器（ViPFormer），以将图像和点云处理统一在单一架构中。 ViPFormer 通过优化模内和跨模态对比目标以无监督的方式学习。然后将预训练的模型转移到各种下游任务，包括 3D 形状分类和语义分割。在不同数据集上的实验表明，ViPFormer 超越了以前最先进的无监督方法，具有更高的准确性、更低的模型复杂性和运行时延迟。最后，ViPFormer 中每个组件的有效性均通过广泛的消融研究得到验证。所提出方法的实现可在 https://github.com/auniquesun/ViPFormer 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种轻量化的视觉与点云联合Transformer模型ViPFormer，用于无监督点云理解任务。
AI.motivation: 现有无监督点云方法（如CrossPoint）存在架构不平衡问题，图像分支体积是点云分支的8.3倍，导致复杂度高且效率低。
AI.method: 设计统一架构的ViPFormer，通过优化模内和跨模对比目标实现无监督学习，并将预训练模型迁移到3D分类和分割等下游任务。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上优于现有无监督方法，准确率更高且模型复杂度和运行时延更低，组件有效性经消融实验验证。
AI.conclusion: ViPFormer通过平衡架构设计有效提升了点云理解的效率和性能，为无监督点云处理提供了新思路。

ai_content.result: 在多个数据集上优于现有无监督方法，准确率更高且模型复杂度和运行时延更低，组件有效性经消融实验验证。
ai_content.conclusion: ViPFormer通过平衡架构设计有效提升了点云理解的效率和性能，为无监督点云处理提供了新思路。
